topic.what: Bloom filter-based source authorization for forward-edge CFI on RISC-V
topic.how: Introduces bld/brl ISA extensions propagating source Section Identifiers (SID) through dedicated BRState register with fixed-probe Bloom filter membership verification at landing sites
topic.why_matters: Closes source-authorization gap left by type-based (admits all same-signature callers) and tag-based (limited by fixed-width register slots) CFI mechanisms; scales to arbitrary authorized sources without capacity constraints

characteristics.what_it_measures: Execution time overhead relative to unprotected baseline
characteristics.evidence: Weighted-cycle evaluation on Spike simulator across 81 BEEBS benchmarks
characteristics.context: brl3 model; 0.633%/1.228% at conservative brl10 model
BRL-CFG provides finer-grained protection at comparable size cost

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average number of permitted targets per indirect call site
characteristics.evidence: Compile-time CFG analysis across 81 BEEBS benchmarks
characteristics.context: Max EC is 9 (wikisort, mergesort); sglib-rbtree/dllist achieve 50% reduction to 1.0

characteristics.evidence: Runtime verification on Spike simulator with Pass/Skip/Fail counts
characteristics.context: Both BRL-Func and BRL-CFG configurations; all latency models

characteristics.evidence: Cycle-by-cycle breakdown: descriptor lookup, hash computation, filter probe, AND-reduce
characteristics.context: bld is 1-cycle; overhead remains below 1.3% even at brl10

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mean number of protected indirect branch targets per benchmark
characteristics.vs_baseline: CFG additionally protects switch jump-table destinations and computed-goto labels
characteristics.context: cover shows 2→181 protected targets under CFG
